Thought for 18s
Mirror Course Archive Images
· powershell
param(
        $attempts++
        try {
            Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $Url -OutFile $DestinationPath -UseBasicParsing
            return "downloaded"
        }
        catch {
            if ($attempts -ge $maxAttempts) {
                throw
            }
            Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
        }
    }
}

$resolvedArchive = Resolve-Path -LiteralPath $ArchiveJsonPath
$resolvedRepoRoot = Resolve-Path -LiteralPath $RepoRoot

$imagesFolderFull = Join-Path $resolvedRepoRoot $ImagesFolderRelativeToRepo
$updatedArchiveFull = Join-Path $resolvedRepoRoot $UpdatedArchiveRelativeToRepo
$manifestFull = Join-Path $resolvedRepoRoot $ManifestRelativeToRepo

Ensure-Folder -Path $imagesFolderFull
Ensure-Folder -Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetDirectoryName($updatedArchiveFull))
Ensure-Folder -Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetDirectoryName($manifestFull))

Write-Info "Loading archive: $resolvedArchive"
$archive = Get-Content -LiteralPath $resolvedArchive -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json -Depth 100

if (-not $archive.courses) {
    throw "Archive does not contain a 'courses' array."
}

$imageRegistry = @{}
$manifestItems = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[object]

foreach ($course in $archive.courses) {
    $courseId = [string]$course.course_id
    Write-Info "Scanning course $courseId"

    foreach ($fieldName in @('original_html', 'lander_html')) {
        $html = $course.$fieldName
        if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($html)) {
            continue
        }

        $matches = Get-ImageMatches -Html $html
        if ($matches.Count -eq 0) {
            continue
        }

        $updatedHtml = $html

        foreach ($match in $matches) {
            $url = $match.Url
            $hash = Get-HashString -Value $url

            if (-not $imageRegistry.ContainsKey($url)) {
                $ext = Get-ExtensionFromUrl -Url $url
                $baseName = "course-$courseId-$fieldName-$hash$ext"
                $safeName = Get-SafeFileName -Name $baseName
                $destPath = Join-Path $imagesFolderFull $safeName
                $relativePath = ($RelativeImagePathPrefix.TrimEnd('/')) + "/" + $safeName

                try {
                    $status = Download-File -Url $url -DestinationPath $destPath -SkipExisting:$SkipExisting -WhatIf:$WhatIf
                    $imageRegistry[$url] = [pscustomobject]@{
                        url           = $url
                        local_filename = $safeName
                        repo_path      = ($ImagesFolderRelativeToRepo.TrimEnd('/')) + "/" + $safeName
                        relative_src   = $relativePath
                        status         = $status
                    }
                }
                catch {
                    Write-WarnMsg "Failed to download $url : $($_.Exception.Message)"
                    $imageRegistry[$url] = [pscustomobject]@{
                        url           = $url
                        local_filename = $null
                        repo_path      = $null
                        relative_src   = $url
                        status         = "failed"
                    }
                }
            }

            $mapped = $imageRegistry[$url]
            $replacement = 'src="' + $mapped.relative_src + '"'
            $escapedUrl = [System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex]::Escape($url)
            $updatedHtml = [System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex]::Replace(
                $updatedHtml,
                'src\s*=\s*["'']' + $escapedUrl + '["'']',
                $replacement,
                [System.Text.RegularExpressions.RegexOptions]::IgnoreCase
            )

            $manifestItems.Add([pscustomobject]@{
                course_id     = $courseId
                field         = $fieldName
                original_url  = $url
                local_filename = $mapped.local_filename
                repo_path     = $mapped.repo_path
                relative_src  = $mapped.relative_src
                status        = $mapped.status
            })
        }

        $course.$fieldName = $updatedHtml
    }
}

$archive.version = if ($archive.version) { [int]$archive.version + 1 } else { 1 }
$archive.image_mirror = [pscustomobject]@{
    generated_utc = (Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString("yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ")
    images_folder = $ImagesFolderRelativeToRepo
    relative_src_prefix = $RelativeImagePathPrefix
    image_count = @($imageRegistry.Keys).Count
}

$archive | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 100 | Set-Content -LiteralPath $updatedArchiveFull -Encoding UTF8

$manifest = [pscustomobject]@{
    generated_utc = (Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString("yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ")
    source_archive = [string]$resolvedArchive
    updated_archive = $UpdatedArchiveRelativeToRepo
    images_folder = $ImagesFolderRelativeToRepo
    image_count = @($imageRegistry.Keys).Count
    items = $manifestItems
}

$manifest | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 100 | Set-Content -LiteralPath $manifestFull -Encoding UTF8

Write-Info "Updated archive written to: $updatedArchiveFull"
Write-Info "Manifest written to: $manifestFull"
Write-Info ("Unique images found: " + @($imageRegistry.Keys).Count)

Write-Host "`nExample usage:" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host 'pwsh ./mirror_course_archive_images.ps1 -ArchiveJsonPath ".\docs\data\course_archive_v2.json" -RepoRoot "."' -ForegroundColor White

Yes. That is the right next move.

I wrote the PowerShell script for exactly that workflow:

scan the archive JSON for image src= references

download each image into a repo data/images folder

rewrite the HTML in the archive to use repo-relative image paths

write an updated archive plus an image manifest

Script
Use this as mirror_course_archive_images.ps1:

param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory = $true)]
    [string]$ArchiveJsonPath,

    [Parameter(Mandatory = $true)]
    [string]$RepoRoot,

    [string]$ImagesFolderRelativeToRepo = "docs/images/course-archive",

    [string]$UpdatedArchiveRelativeToRepo = "docs/data/course_archive_mirrored.json",

    [string]$ManifestRelativeToRepo = "docs/data/course_image_manifest.json",

    [string]$RelativeImagePathPrefix = "../images/course-archive",

    [switch]$SkipExisting,

    [switch]$WhatIf
)

Set-StrictMode -Version Latest
$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"

function Write-Info {
    param([string]$Message)
    Write-Host "[INFO] $Message" -ForegroundColor Cyan
}

function Write-WarnMsg {
    param([string]$Message)
    Write-Host "[WARN] $Message" -ForegroundColor Yellow
}

function Get-SafeFileName {
    param([string]$Name)

    $safe = $Name.ToLowerInvariant()
    $safe = $safe -replace '%20', '-'
    $safe = $safe -replace '[^a-z0-9._-]', '-'
    $safe = $safe -replace '-{2,}', '-'
    $safe = $safe.Trim('-')

    if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($safe)) {
        return "file"
    }

    return $safe
}

function Get-ExtensionFromUrl {
    param([string]$Url)

    try {
        $uri = [System.Uri]$Url
        $leaf = [System.IO.Path]::GetFileName($uri.AbsolutePath)
        $ext = [System.IO.Path]::GetExtension($leaf)
        if (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($ext)) {
            return $ext.ToLowerInvariant()
        }
    }
    catch {
    }

    return ".bin"
}

function Get-HashString {
    param([string]$Value)

    $sha = [System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256]::Create()
    try {
        $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($Value)
        $hashBytes = $sha.ComputeHash($bytes)
        $hash = [System.BitConverter]::ToString($hashBytes).Replace("-", "").ToLowerInvariant()
        return $hash.Substring(0, 12)
    }
    finally {
        $sha.Dispose()
    }
}

function Get-ImageMatches {
    param([string]$Html)

    if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Html)) {
        return @()
    }

    $pattern = 'src\s*=\s*["''](?<url>[^"'']+)["'']'
    $matches = [System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex]::Matches(
        $Html,
        $pattern,
        [System.Text.RegularExpressions.RegexOptions]::IgnoreCase
    )

    $results = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[object]
    foreach ($match in $matches) {
        $url = $match.Groups['url'].Value
        if ($url -match '^https?://') {
            $results.Add([pscustomobject]@{
                Original = $match.Value
                Url      = $url
            })
        }
    }

    return $results
}

function Ensure-Folder {
    param([string]$Path)

    if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $Path)) {
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $Path -Force | Out-Null
    }
}

function Download-File {
    param(
        [string]$Url,
        [string]$DestinationPath,
        [switch]$SkipExisting,
        [switch]$WhatIf
    )

    if ($SkipExisting -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $DestinationPath)) {
        return "skipped-existing"
    }

    if ($WhatIf) {
        return "whatif"
    }

    $attempts = 0
    $maxAttempts = 3
    while ($true) {
        $attempts++
        try {
            Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $Url -OutFile $DestinationPath -UseBasicParsing
            return "downloaded"
        }
        catch {
            if ($attempts -ge $maxAttempts) {
                throw
            }
            Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
        }
    }
}

$resolvedArchive = Resolve-Path -LiteralPath $ArchiveJsonPath
$resolvedRepoRoot = Resolve-Path -LiteralPath $RepoRoot

$imagesFolderFull = Join-Path $resolvedRepoRoot $ImagesFolderRelativeToRepo
$updatedArchiveFull = Join-Path $resolvedRepoRoot $UpdatedArchiveRelativeToRepo
$manifestFull = Join-Path $resolvedRepoRoot $ManifestRelativeToRepo

Ensure-Folder -Path $imagesFolderFull
Ensure-Folder -Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetDirectoryName($updatedArchiveFull))
Ensure-Folder -Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetDirectoryName($manifestFull))

Write-Info "Loading archive: $resolvedArchive"
$archive = Get-Content -LiteralPath $resolvedArchive -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json -Depth 100

if (-not $archive.courses) {
    throw "Archive does not contain a 'courses' array."
}

$imageRegistry = @{}
$manifestItems = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[object]

foreach ($course in $archive.courses) {
    $courseId = [string]$course.course_id
    Write-Info "Scanning course $courseId"

    foreach ($fieldName in @('original_html', 'lander_html')) {
        $html = $course.$fieldName
        if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($html)) {
            continue
        }

        $matches = Get-ImageMatches -Html $html
        if ($matches.Count -eq 0) {
            continue
        }

        $updatedHtml = $html

        foreach ($match in $matches) {
            $url = $match.Url
            $hash = Get-HashString -Value $url

            if (-not $imageRegistry.ContainsKey($url)) {
                $ext = Get-ExtensionFromUrl -Url $url
                $baseName = "course-$courseId-$fieldName-$hash$ext"
                $safeName = Get-SafeFileName -Name $baseName
                $destPath = Join-Path $imagesFolderFull $safeName
                $relativePath = ($RelativeImagePathPrefix.TrimEnd('/')) + "/" + $safeName

                try {
                    $status = Download-File -Url $url -DestinationPath $destPath -SkipExisting:$SkipExisting -WhatIf:$WhatIf
                    $imageRegistry[$url] = [pscustomobject]@{
                        url            = $url
                        local_filename = $safeName
                        repo_path      = ($ImagesFolderRelativeToRepo.TrimEnd('/')) + "/" + $safeName
                        relative_src   = $relativePath
                        status         = $status
                    }
                }
                catch {
                    Write-WarnMsg "Failed to download $url : $($_.Exception.Message)"
                    $imageRegistry[$url] = [pscustomobject]@{
                        url            = $url
                        local_filename = $null
                        repo_path      = $null
                        relative_src   = $url
                        status         = "failed"
                    }
                }
            }

            $mapped = $imageRegistry[$url]
            $replacement = 'src="' + $mapped.relative_src + '"'
            $escapedUrl = [System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex]::Escape($url)

            $updatedHtml = [System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex]::Replace(
                $updatedHtml,
                'src\s*=\s*["'']' + $escapedUrl + '["'']',
                $replacement,
                [System.Text.RegularExpressions.RegexOptions]::IgnoreCase
            )

            $manifestItems.Add([pscustomobject]@{
                course_id      = $courseId
                field          = $fieldName
                original_url   = $url
                local_filename = $mapped.local_filename
                repo_path      = $mapped.repo_path
                relative_src   = $mapped.relative_src
                status         = $mapped.status
            })
        }

        $course.$fieldName = $updatedHtml
    }
}

$archive.version = if ($archive.version) { [int]$archive.version + 1 } else { 1 }
$archive.image_mirror = [pscustomobject]@{
    generated_utc       = (Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString("yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ")
    images_folder       = $ImagesFolderRelativeToRepo
    relative_src_prefix = $RelativeImagePathPrefix
    image_count         = @($imageRegistry.Keys).Count
}

$archive | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 100 | Set-Content -LiteralPath $updatedArchiveFull -Encoding UTF8

$manifest = [pscustomobject]@{
    generated_utc  = (Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString("yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ")
    source_archive = [string]$resolvedArchive
    updated_archive = $UpdatedArchiveRelativeToRepo
    images_folder  = $ImagesFolderRelativeToRepo
    image_count    = @($imageRegistry.Keys).Count
    items          = $manifestItems
}

$manifest | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 100 | Set-Content -LiteralPath $manifestFull -Encoding UTF8

Write-Info "Updated archive written to: $updatedArchiveFull"
Write-Info "Manifest written to: $manifestFull"
Write-Info ("Unique images found: " + @($imageRegistry.Keys).Count)

Write-Host "`nExample usage:" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host 'pwsh ./mirror_course_archive_images.ps1 -ArchiveJsonPath ".\docs\data\course_archive_v2.json" -RepoRoot "."' -ForegroundColor White

  -ArchiveJsonPath ".\docs\data\course_archive_v2.json" `
  -RepoRoot "."

Supply values for the following parameters:

[INFO] Loading archive: D:\Users\ten77\Documents\GitHub\910cpr-class-landers\raw\course_archive_v1.json

: The property 'Count' cannot be found on this object. Verify that the property exists.
PS D:\Users\ten77\Documents\GitHub\910cpr-class-landers>

Good. You ran it correctly. 👍
The error tells us exactly where the script broke.

What happened
PowerShell sometimes returns a single object instead of an array when there is only one match.

So this line fails when there is only one <img> tag:

if ($matches.Count -eq 0)
Because $matches becomes a single object, not a collection.

Classic PowerShell quirk.

Fix (simple and safe)
Find this line in the script:

$matches = Get-ImageMatches -Html $html
Replace it with:

$matches = @(Get-ImageMatches -Html $html)
The @() forces PowerShell to always treat it as an array, even if there's only one result.

Then change this block
Replace:

if ($matches.Count -eq 0) {
    continue
}
with:

if ($matches.Count -eq 0) {
    continue
}
(no change needed once @() is used)

Why this fix works
@() forces PowerShell to treat results as:

0 items  -> empty array
1 item   -> array with 1 element
many     -> array
Without it PowerShell does this:

0 items  -> $null
1 item   -> object
many     -> array
Which breaks .Count.

After the fix
Run again:

pwsh ./scripts/mirror_course_archive_images.ps1 `
-ArchiveJsonPath "d:\Users\ten77\Documents\GitHub\910cpr-class-landers\raw\course_archive_v1.json" `
-RepoRoot "d:\Users\ten77\Documents\GitHub\910cpr-class-landers"
You should start seeing:

[INFO] Scanning course 209806
[INFO] Scanning course 344486
[INFO] Scanning course 448630
Then images begin downloading.

Small improvement I recommend next
Once this runs successfully, we should add one more protection:

User-Agent header
because some sites block Invoke-WebRequest.

Takes 3 lines to harden the downloader.

If you'd like, I can also give you the fully corrected v2 of the script so you don't have to patch anything manually. It will also:

• download faster
• avoid duplicate images
• retry failures
• produce cleaner filenames for SEO.

And it will make the 26,000 lander build smoother.

Yes. That explains it.

Your current image script is almost certainly walking:

/docs/classes/*.html
So it mirrored the already-built session pages, which mainly contain location/client imagery, not the course archive HTML.

The fix is to use a script that reads:

/raw/course_archive_v2.json
extracts <img src="..."> from both:

original_html

lander_html

downloads those files into your repo, and rewrites the JSON to use local repo-relative paths.

Below is the full file for that job.

Save this as:

scripts/mirror_course_archive_images.py
Full file
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import hashlib
import json
import os
import re
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any
from urllib.parse import urlparse, unquote

import requests


IMG_SRC_RE = re.compile(r"""src\s*=\s*["']([^"']+)["']""", re.IGNORECASE)


def log(msg: str) -> None:
    print(f"[INFO] {msg}")


def warn(msg: str) -> None:
    print(f"[WARN] {msg}")


def safe_slug(value: str) -> str:
    value = value.lower()
    value = value.replace("%20", "-")
    value = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9._-]+", "-", value)
    value = re.sub(r"-{2,}", "-", value)
    return value.strip("-") or "file"


def short_hash(value: str, length: int = 12) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(value.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:length]


def extract_img_urls(html: str | None) -> list[str]:
    if not html:
        return []
    urls = []
    for match in IMG_SRC_RE.findall(html):
        if match.lower().startswith(("http://", "https://")):
            urls.append(match)
    return urls


def infer_extension(url: str, content_type: str | None = None) -> str:
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    path = unquote(parsed.path)
    ext = Path(path).suffix.lower()

    if ext in {".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png", ".gif", ".webp", ".svg"}:
        return ext

    if content_type:
        ct = content_type.lower().split(";")[0].strip()
        mapping = {
            "image/jpeg": ".jpg",
            "image/jpg": ".jpg",
            "image/png": ".png",
            "image/gif": ".gif",
            "image/webp": ".webp",
            "image/svg+xml": ".svg",
        }
        if ct in mapping:
            return mapping[ct]

    return ".bin"


def build_local_filename(
    course_id: str,
    field_name: str,
    url: str,
    content_type: str | None = None,
) -> str:
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    original_name = Path(unquote(parsed.path)).name or "image"
    original_stem = Path(original_name).stem or "image"
    ext = infer_extension(url, content_type=content_type)
    hashed = short_hash(url)
    raw_name = f"course-{course_id}-{field_name}-{original_stem}-{hashed}{ext}"
    return safe_slug(raw_name)


def download_file(
    url: str,
    destination: Path,
    timeout: int = 30,
    retries: int = 3,
    skip_existing: bool = False,
) -> tuple[str, str | None]:
    if skip_existing and destination.exists():
        return "skipped-existing", None

    headers = {
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) course-archive-mirror/1.0"
    }

    last_error = None
    for attempt in range(1, retries + 1):
        try:
            response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=timeout, stream=True)
            response.raise_for_status()

            destination.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            with destination.open("wb") as f:
                for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):
                    if chunk:
                        f.write(chunk)

            return "downloaded", response.headers.get("Content-Type")
        except Exception as exc:
            last_error = str(exc)
            if attempt < retries:
                time.sleep(1)

    raise RuntimeError(f"Failed after {retries} attempts: {url} :: {last_error}")


def replace_src_urls(html: str | None, replacements: dict[str, str]) -> str | None:
    if not html:
        return html

    updated = html
    for old_url, new_src in replacements.items():
        pattern = re.compile(
            r"""src\s*=\s*["']""" + re.escape(old_url) + r"""["']""",
            re.IGNORECASE,
        )
        updated = pattern.sub(f'src="{new_src}"', updated)

    return updated


def load_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        return json.load(f)


def save_json(path: Path, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Mirror course archive HTML images locally and rewrite image paths in JSON."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--archive-json",
        required=True,
        help="Path to the source course archive JSON, e.g. raw/course_archive_v2.json",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--repo-root",
        required=True,
        help="Path to repo root, e.g. D:/Users/.../910cpr-class-landers",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--images-dir",
        default="docs/images/course-archive",
        help="Repo-relative folder where mirrored images should be stored.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--updated-archive",
        default="docs/data/course_archive_mirrored.json",
        help="Repo-relative output path for the updated archive JSON.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--manifest",
        default="docs/data/course_image_manifest.json",
        help="Repo-relative output path for the image manifest JSON.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--relative-prefix",
        default="../images/course-archive",
        help="HTML-relative src prefix to write into course HTML fields.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--skip-existing",
        action="store_true",
        help="Skip downloading files that already exist locally.",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    repo_root = Path(args.repo_root).resolve()
    archive_json = Path(args.archive_json).resolve()
    images_dir = (repo_root / args.images_dir).resolve()
    updated_archive_path = (repo_root / args.updated_archive).resolve()
    manifest_path = (repo_root / args.manifest).resolve()

    if not archive_json.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Archive JSON not found: {archive_json}")

    log(f"Loading archive: {archive_json}")
    archive = load_json(archive_json)

    courses = archive.get("courses")
    if not isinstance(courses, list):
        raise ValueError("Archive JSON must contain a top-level 'courses' list.")

    images_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    updated_archive_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    manifest_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    global_registry: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
    manifest_items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

    for course in courses:
        course_id = str(course.get("course_id", "unknown"))
        log(f"Scanning course {course_id}")

        for field_name in ("original_html", "lander_html"):
            html = course.get(field_name)
            urls = extract_img_urls(html)

            if not urls:
                continue

            replacements: dict[str, str] = {}

            for url in urls:
                if url not in global_registry:
                    try:
                        temp_name = build_local_filename(course_id, field_name, url, None)
                        temp_path = images_dir / temp_name
                        status, content_type = download_file(
                            url=url,
                            destination=temp_path,
                            skip_existing=args.skip_existing,
                        )

                        final_name = build_local_filename(course_id, field_name, url, content_type)
                        final_path = images_dir / final_name

                        if temp_path != final_path:
                            if final_path.exists():
                                temp_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
                            else:
                                temp_path.rename(final_path)

                        repo_rel_path = Path(args.images_dir.replace("\\", "/")) / final_name
                        relative_src = f"{args.relative_prefix.rstrip('/')}/{final_name}"

                        global_registry[url] = {
                            "original_url": url,
                            "local_filename": final_name,
                            "repo_path": str(repo_rel_path).replace("\\", "/"),
                            "relative_src": relative_src,
                            "status": status,
                            "content_type": content_type,
                        }
                    except Exception as exc:
                        warn(f"Failed to download {url} :: {exc}")
                        global_registry[url] = {
                            "original_url": url,
                            "local_filename": None,
                            "repo_path": None,
                            "relative_src": url,
                            "status": "failed",
                            "content_type": None,
                            "error": str(exc),
                        }

                mapped = global_registry[url]
                replacements[url] = mapped["relative_src"]

                manifest_items.append(
                    {
                        "course_id": course_id,
                        "field": field_name,
                        "original_url": url,
                        "local_filename": mapped.get("local_filename"),
                        "repo_path": mapped.get("repo_path"),
                        "relative_src": mapped.get("relative_src"),
                        "status": mapped.get("status"),
                    }
                )

            course[field_name] = replace_src_urls(html, replacements)

    old_version = archive.get("version")
    if isinstance(old_version, int):
        archive["version"] = old_version + 1
    else:
        archive["version"] = 1

    archive["image_mirror"] = {
        "source_archive": str(archive_json),
        "generated_utc": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime()),
        "images_dir": args.images_dir,
        "relative_prefix": args.relative_prefix,
        "unique_image_count": len(global_registry),
    }

    manifest = {
        "generated_utc": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime()),
        "source_archive": str(archive_json),
        "updated_archive": str(updated_archive_path),
        "images_dir": args.images_dir,
        "unique_image_count": len(global_registry),
        "items": manifest_items,
    }

    save_json(updated_archive_path, archive)
    save_json(manifest_path, manifest)

    log(f"Updated archive written to: {updated_archive_path}")
    log(f"Manifest written to: {manifest_path}")
    log(f"Unique images found: {len(global_registry)}")

    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

Which makes finishing the framework much faster.

mirror_images.py
Python
okay I have it in repo RAW... how do we get this script to use v3 also?


Thought for 4 seconds
Right now, mirror_images.py does not read any archive JSON at all. It only walks docs/ and rewrites existing .html files, which is why it grabbed class/location images and ignored the course archive. You can see that in the current script: it sets DOCS_DIR, scans .html files under it, and never opens course_archive_v3.json. 
mirror_images


So the fix is not a tiny parameter tweak. It needs a full-file replacement so it can do both jobs:

mirror images from raw/course_archive_v3.json

rewrite image paths inside the archive HTML

optionally still mirror built .html pages afterward

Replace scripts/mirror_images.py with this
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import hashlib
import json
import os
import re
import shutil
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any
from urllib.parse import unquote, urlparse

import requests


TIMEOUT = 20
IMG_SRC_RE = re.compile(r"""src\s*=\s*["']([^"']+)["']""", re.IGNORECASE)


def log(msg: str) -> None:
    print(f"[INFO] {msg}")


def warn(msg: str) -> None:
    print(f"[WARN] {msg}")


def safe_slug(value: str) -> str:
    value = value.lower()
    value = value.replace("%20", "-")
    value = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9._-]+", "-", value)
    value = re.sub(r"-{2,}", "-", value)
    return value.strip("-") or "file"


def short_hash(value: str, length: int = 12) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(value.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:length]


def load_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        return json.load(f)


def save_json(path: Path, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)


def extract_remote_img_urls(html: str | None) -> list[str]:
    if not html:
        return []
    urls = []
    for match in IMG_SRC_RE.findall(html):
        if match.lower().startswith(("http://", "https://")):
            urls.append(match)
    return urls


def infer_extension(url: str, content_type: str | None = None) -> str:
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    path = unquote(parsed.path)
    ext = Path(path).suffix.lower()

    if ext in {".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png", ".gif", ".webp", ".svg"}:
        return ext

    if content_type:
        ct = content_type.lower().split(";")[0].strip()
        mapping = {
            "image/jpeg": ".jpg",
            "image/jpg": ".jpg",
            "image/png": ".png",
            "image/gif": ".gif",
            "image/webp": ".webp",
            "image/svg+xml": ".svg",
        }
        if ct in mapping:
            return mapping[ct]

    return ".bin"


def build_local_filename(
    course_id: str,
    field_name: str,
    url: str,
    content_type: str | None = None,
) -> str:
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    original_name = Path(unquote(parsed.path)).name or "image"
    original_stem = Path(original_name).stem or "image"
    ext = infer_extension(url, content_type=content_type)
    hashed = short_hash(url)
    raw_name = f"course-{course_id}-{field_name}-{original_stem}-{hashed}{ext}"
    return safe_slug(raw_name)


def download_file(
    url: str,
    destination: Path,
    timeout: int = TIMEOUT,
    retries: int = 3,
    skip_existing: bool = False,
) -> tuple[str, str | None]:
    if skip_existing and destination.exists():
        return "skipped-existing", None

    headers = {
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) 910cpr-image-mirror/1.0"
    }

    last_error = None
    for attempt in range(1, retries + 1):
        try:
            response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=timeout, stream=True)
            response.raise_for_status()

            destination.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            with destination.open("wb") as f:
                for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):
                    if chunk:
                        f.write(chunk)

            return "downloaded", response.headers.get("Content-Type")
        except Exception as exc:
            last_error = str(exc)
            if attempt < retries:
                time.sleep(1)

    raise RuntimeError(f"Failed after {retries} attempts: {url} :: {last_error}")


def replace_src_urls(html: str | None, replacements: dict[str, str]) -> str | None:
    if not html:
        return html

    updated = html
    for old_url, new_src in replacements.items():
        pattern = re.compile(
            r"""src\s*=\s*["']""" + re.escape(old_url) + r"""["']""",
            re.IGNORECASE,
        )
        updated = pattern.sub(f'src="{new_src}"', updated)

    return updated


def mirror_course_archive_images(
    repo_root: Path,
    archive_json: Path,
    images_dir_rel: str = "docs/images/course-archive",
    updated_archive_rel: str = "docs/data/course_archive_mirrored.json",
    manifest_rel: str = "docs/data/course_image_manifest.json",
    relative_prefix: str = "../images/course-archive",
    skip_existing: bool = False,
) -> tuple[Path, Path, int]:
    log(f"Loading archive: {archive_json}")
    archive = load_json(archive_json)

    courses = archive.get("courses")
    if not isinstance(courses, list):
        raise ValueError("Archive JSON must contain a top-level 'courses' list.")

    images_dir = (repo_root / images_dir_rel).resolve()
    updated_archive_path = (repo_root / updated_archive_rel).resolve()
    manifest_path = (repo_root / manifest_rel).resolve()

    images_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    updated_archive_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    manifest_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    global_registry: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
    manifest_items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

    for course in courses:
        course_id = str(course.get("course_id", "unknown"))
        log(f"Scanning archive course {course_id}")

        for field_name in ("raw_enrollware_html", "original_html", "lander_html"):
            html = course.get(field_name)
            urls = extract_remote_img_urls(html)

            if not urls:
                continue

            replacements: dict[str, str] = {}

            for url in urls:
                if url not in global_registry:
                    try:
                        temp_name = build_local_filename(course_id, field_name, url, None)
                        temp_path = images_dir / temp_name
                        status, content_type = download_file(
                            url=url,
                            destination=temp_path,
                            skip_existing=skip_existing,
                        )

                        final_name = build_local_filename(course_id, field_name, url, content_type)
                        final_path = images_dir / final_name

                        if temp_path != final_path:
                            if final_path.exists():
                                temp_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
                            else:
                                temp_path.rename(final_path)

                        repo_rel_path = Path(images_dir_rel.replace("\\", "/")) / final_name
                        relative_src = f"{relative_prefix.rstrip('/')}/{final_name}"

                        global_registry[url] = {
                            "original_url": url,
                            "local_filename": final_name,
                            "repo_path": str(repo_rel_path).replace("\\", "/"),
                            "relative_src": relative_src,
                            "status": status,
                            "content_type": content_type,
                        }
                    except Exception as exc:
                        warn(f"Failed to download {url} :: {exc}")
                        global_registry[url] = {
                            "original_url": url,
                            "local_filename": None,
                            "repo_path": None,
                            "relative_src": url,
                            "status": "failed",
                            "content_type": None,
                            "error": str(exc),
                        }

                mapped = global_registry[url]
                replacements[url] = mapped["relative_src"]

                manifest_items.append(
                    {
                        "course_id": course_id,
                        "field": field_name,
                        "original_url": url,
                        "local_filename": mapped.get("local_filename"),
                        "repo_path": mapped.get("repo_path"),
                        "relative_src": mapped.get("relative_src"),
                        "status": mapped.get("status"),
                    }
                )

            course[field_name] = replace_src_urls(html, replacements)

    old_version = archive.get("version")
    archive["version"] = old_version + 1 if isinstance(old_version, int) else 1
    archive["image_mirror"] = {
        "source_archive": str(archive_json),
        "generated_utc": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime()),
        "images_dir": images_dir_rel,
        "relative_prefix": relative_prefix,
        "unique_image_count": len(global_registry),
    }

    manifest = {
        "generated_utc": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime()),
        "source_archive": str(archive_json),
        "updated_archive": str(updated_archive_path),
        "images_dir": images_dir_rel,
        "unique_image_count": len(global_registry),
        "items": manifest_items,
    }

    save_json(updated_archive_path, archive)
    save_json(manifest_path, manifest)

    log(f"Updated archive written to: {updated_archive_path}")
    log(f"Manifest written to: {manifest_path}")
    log(f"Unique course-archive images found: {len(global_registry)}")

    return updated_archive_path, manifest_path, len(global_registry)


def download_html_image(url: str, image_dir: Path, skip_existing: bool = False) -> str:
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    filename = os.path.basename(parsed.path) or f"image-{short_hash(url)}.jpg"
    filename = safe_slug(filename)
    destination = image_dir / filename

    if skip_existing and destination.exists():
        return f"/images/{filename}"

    headers = {
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) 910cpr-image-mirror/1.0"
    }

    response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=TIMEOUT)
    response.raise_for_status()

    with destination.open("wb") as f:
        f.write(response.content)

    log(f"Downloaded page image: {filename}")
    return f"/images/{filename}"


def process_html_file(path: Path, image_dir: Path, skip_existing: bool = False) -> None:
    html = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    changed = False

    matches = IMG_SRC_RE.findall(html)
    for url in matches:
        if not url.lower().startswith(("http://", "https://")):
            continue

        try:
            local = download_html_image(url, image_dir, skip_existing=skip_existing)
            if local != url:
                html = html.replace(url, local)
                changed = True
        except Exception as exc:
            warn(f"Failed page image {url} in {path}: {exc}")

    if changed:
        path.write_text(html, encoding="utf-8")
        log(f"Updated HTML page: {path}")


def mirror_existing_html_pages(repo_root: Path, skip_existing: bool = False) -> None:
    docs_dir = repo_root / "docs"
    image_dir = docs_dir / "images"
    image_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    log("Scanning built HTML pages under docs/")
    for root, _, files in os.walk(docs_dir):
        for file in files:
            if file.endswith(".html"):
                process_html_file(Path(root) / file, image_dir, skip_existing=skip_existing)
    log("Finished scanning built HTML pages.")


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Mirror images from course archive JSON and/or built HTML pages."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--repo-root",
        default=".",
        help="Repo root path. Default: current directory",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--archive-json",
        default="raw/course_archive_v3.json",
        help="Path to source course archive JSON. Default: raw/course_archive_v3.json",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--skip-existing",
        action="store_true",
        help="Skip images that already exist locally.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--pages-only",
        action="store_true",
        help="Only mirror existing built HTML pages under docs/.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--archive-only",
        action="store_true",
        help="Only mirror images from the course archive JSON.",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    repo_root = Path(args.repo_root).resolve()
    archive_json = (repo_root / args.archive_json).resolve() if not Path(args.archive_json).is_absolute() else Path(args.archive_json).resolve()

    if not args.pages_only:
        mirror_course_archive_images(
            repo_root=repo_root,
            archive_json=archive_json,
            skip_existing=args.skip_existing,
        )

    if not args.archive_only:
        mirror_existing_html_pages(
            repo_root=repo_root,
            skip_existing=args.skip_existing,
        )

    log("Done.")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
How to run it for v3
From repo root:

python .\scripts\mirror_images.py --archive-json raw\course_archive_v3.json --repo-root . --archive-only
That does only the course-archive mirroring first, which is what you want right now.

What it will create
It will populate:

docs/images/course-archive/
and write:

docs/data/course_archive_mirrored.json
docs/data/course_image_manifest.json
Then, if you want, run the page scan too
Later, when you want to also localize images inside already-built HTML pages:

python .\scripts\mirror_images.py --archive-json raw\course_archive_v3.json --repo-root . --skip-existing
That will:

mirror archive images

then scan docs/*.html

What to send me after you run it
Paste:

the console output

and this count:

(dir .\docs\images\course-archive).Count
That’ll tell us immediately whether the AHA / ARC / HSI / course graphics finally came down.

PS D:\Users\ten77\Documents\GitHub\910cpr-class-landers>

Yeah, that was underwhelming.

But the script probably did work correctly. The disappointing part is the archive content, not the mirroring logic.

What that output really means
It scanned all 23 courses and found only 16 unique remote images.

That usually means one or more of these are true:

a lot of course entries in v3 do not actually contain <img src=...> HTML

several courses reuse the same logo/image URLs, so 23 courses does not mean 23+ images

some courses only have cleaned lander HTML with no images preserved

some of the raw Enrollware HTML you wanted preserved never made it into the archive fields

That matches your complaint from earlier, unfortunately.

Translation
The script did not fail.

It successfully proved:

the archive only contains 16 unique image references worth downloading

Which is why the result feels weak.

The real issue
The problem is almost certainly:

course_archive_v3.json is still not rich enough in raw HTML/image content.

So we are mirroring from a source that is already partially stripped down.

Quick proof
Run this:

Get-Content .\docs\data\course_image_manifest.json -TotalCount 200
That will show exactly which 16 URLs it found.

And run this too:

(dir .\docs\images\course-archive).Count
I expect that number to be close to 16.

What I think happened
A lot of your v3 entries probably contain things like:

strong text

rewritten course descriptions

metadata

maybe one logo

But not the full ugly Enrollware HTML blocks with all the embedded course graphics you were trying to preserve.

So the script mirrored faithfully from a thin archive.

The honest next move
We need to inspect one course in v3 directly and compare it to what you originally pasted.

Pick one that should obviously have several images, like:

210549

252737

344486

253588

Then run:

Select-String -Path .\raw\course_archive_v3.json -Pattern '210549|img|src='
Better yet, open the JSON and inspect one course block.

If that course in v3 only has one or zero <img> references, then we’ve proven the archive is the bottleneck.

My blunt read
You were right to be annoyed earlier.

This result strongly suggests the archive still does not fully preserve the source HTML richness you expected.

So the next job is not more mirroring.

The next job is:

audit v3 for missing raw HTML/image density, then rebuild the weak entries.

Paste the first 100–150 lines of docs\data\course_image_manifest.json, and I’ll tell you exactly what got mirrored and what clearly didn’t.

<div style="text-align: center; background-color: #3a5289; color: white; padding: 10px; font-weight: bold !important;">AHA BLS Provider &ndash; In-Person &ndash; Renewal</div>

<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;">This American Heart Association (AHA) Basic Life Support (BLS) Renewal course is designed for healthcare professionals with a current, valid AHA BLS Provider card who are seeking recertification. This course provides a focused review of core CPR and AED skills and allows participants to demonstrate continued competency in high-quality CPR.</p>
<!-- Prerequisite -->
<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;"><strong>Requirement:</strong> You must bring a current, valid AHA BLS Provider card to class. If your card is expired, please contact us prior to registration to verify eligibility.</p>
<!-- Ensure Container Doesn't Collapse -->
<div style="clear: both;">&nbsp;</div>
<!-- Course Style -->
<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;">This instructor-led, classroom-based course includes video instruction, skills demonstration, and scenario-based practice. Instructor-to-student ratio is maintained at 1:6 during hands-on sessions to meet AHA standards.</p>
<!-- Quick Reference -->
<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;">We recommend reviewing the <strong><a style="color: #005184 !important; text-decoration: none !important;" href="https://www.enrollware.com/sitefiles/coastalcprtraining/CCC%20CLASS%20FORMS/2020BLSSummary%5B1%5D.pdf">BLS Quick Reference Guide</a></strong> prior to class.</p>
<!-- Course Highlights -->
<p style="text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;"><strong>Course Highlights:</strong></p>
<ul style="text-indent: 0px !important; margin-left: 15px !important;">
<li>High-quality CPR for adults, children, and infants</li>
<li>Use of AED and relief of choking</li>
<li>Effective team dynamics and communication</li>
<li>Opioid emergencies and maternity considerations</li>
<li>Hands-on practice with BVM, pocket mask, and two-rescuer CPR</li>
</ul>

<!-- Certification -->

<!-- CME Info - Retained -->

<p style="padding: 10px; text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;"><strong>Continuing Medical Education (CME) Credits:</strong><br /> The American Heart Association (AHA) authorizes the use of their courses for continuing education credits. The number of credit hours depends on the class and the accrediting agency.</p>
<p style="padding: 10px; text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;"><strong>Accrediting Agencies:</strong><br /> - American Medical Association (AMA): 1 credit = 1 hour.<br /> - American Nurses Credentialing Center (ANCC): 1 credit = 1 hour.<br /> - Commission on Accreditation for Pre-Hospital Continuing Education (CAPCE): 1 credit = 1 hour.</p>
<p style="padding: 10px; text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;"><strong>Credit Hours for AHA Courses:</strong><br /> - <strong>BLS Online:</strong> 0.25 credits per hour.<br /> - <strong>ACLS / PALS Online:</strong> 0.5 credits per hour.<br /> - <strong>In-Person Courses:</strong> Typically do not offer CME credits, except for specific regional requirements.</p>
<p style="padding: 10px; text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;">For more detailed information about how to claim your CME credits, refer to the official AHA guidelines or contact your licensing agency for specific requirements.</p>
</div>
</div>

Thanks for sharing the Enrollware course configuration. I reviewed it carefully. Overall, it’s very strong and professionally structured, but there are a few areas where you could improve conversion, compliance clarity, and SEO performance.

Below is a structured review with what’s good, what to fix, and what to consider improving.

Overall Assessment
Quality: Very good
Compliance: Good (AHA requirements are properly referenced)
Conversion: Good but could be improved
SEO: Good but slightly long

Your course page already follows the “class page = product page” model that modern training sites should use—where students land directly on the class they want and book immediately. 
HOVN SITREP


That’s exactly the direction Google search and booking behavior is moving toward.

What You Did Well
1. Clear Course Identity
You clearly identify:

Course type

Provider (AHA)

Certification

Course length

Requirements

Hands-on components

This is exactly what healthcare students want to see quickly.

2. Good Use of Visual Layout
Your header + image formatting works well:

Banner

Right-aligned course photo

Short readable paragraphs

This keeps the page scannable on mobile.

3. Strong Add-On Structure
Your manual compliance prompt is excellent.

It clearly explains the AHA requirement:

Students must have access to the current manual before, during, and after class.

This protects you if audited.

You also correctly provide three compliance pathways:

• Already have manual
• Buy from AHA
• Buy from you

This is exactly how AHA prefers it handled.

4. Good Skill List
Your highlights cover the right BLS competencies:

High-quality CPR

AED use

Airway management

Team dynamics

Opioid emergencies

The opioid component is especially relevant because newer guidance emphasizes overdose response training. 
Highlights_2024FA_241118


Issues You Should Fix
1. Add-On Formatting Glitch
This item is broken:

AHA-SKILLS-HS_FA - <br><br>A<img ...
HA Heartsaver® First Aid [AHA]
The text splits the word AHA.

Should be:

<img src="..."> AHA Heartsaver® First Aid
Right now it looks messy.

2. Manual eBook Description Tag Error
You have:

</b></i>
But no opening <b> tag.

Fix to:

</i>
or

<strong>
Minor but worth cleaning.

3. Course Length Statement
You wrote:

Approximately 3 hours (minimum per AHA PAM for BLS Renewal)

Most students don’t know what PAM means.

Better:

Approximately 3 hours (AHA minimum instructional time for renewal courses).
Cleaner and clearer.

4. Requirement Language Could Be Stronger
Current:

If your card is expired, please contact us prior to registration to verify eligibility.

Better (less friction):

If your card is expired, contact us before registering so we can determine whether a renewal or full BLS course is required.
This reduces confusion.

Conversion Improvements (High Impact)
Add a Clear Call-to-Action Near the Top
Right now you rely on the Enrollware button outside the description.

Inside the description add:

<strong>Reserve your seat now using the schedule below.</strong>
Simple but increases conversions.

Add Target Audience
Healthcare buyers scan quickly.

Add:

This course is intended for:

• Nurses
• Physicians
• EMTs and paramedics
• Dental professionals
• Healthcare students
• Allied health personnel
This improves SEO too.

Add What to Bring
Students often ask.

Add section:

What to Bring

• Current AHA BLS Provider card
• Access to the BLS Provider Manual
• Comfortable clothing for hands-on practice
Reduces support emails.

SEO Improvements
Your SEO description is very good, but slightly long.

Current ~460 characters.

Better version (~280 characters):

Renew your American Heart Association BLS Provider certification with our fast, hands-on renewal course for healthcare professionals. Learn high-quality CPR, AED use, airway management, and team resuscitation skills. Receive your AHA BLS eCard the same day.

Cleaner for search results.

Minor Technical Suggestions
Use consistent course naming
Current title:

AHA - BLS Provider - In-person Renewal
Better:

AHA BLS Provider Renewal (In-Person)
This matches how people search.

Consider adding these keywords
For SEO:

BLS renewal near me

AHA BLS renewal

healthcare CPR renewal

BLS recertification

One Strategic Suggestion
Create two versions of this course page:

1️⃣ BLS Provider (Full Course)
2️⃣ BLS Renewal

Many providers combine them, but separate pages rank better in Google.

Final Score
Category	Score
Content Quality	9/10
Compliance	9/10
SEO	8/10
Conversion	8/10
Structure	9/10
Overall: 8.7 / 10

This is already a very strong class page.

The #1 layout CPR training companies use to fill classes faster

AHA-safe wording that protects you during Training Center audits

Just say and I’ll build it.

<!-- Course Overview -->
<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;">The American Heart Association (AHA) Heartcode BLS course uses a blended learning format that includes an interactive online course followed by an in-person skills session. This format is ideal for healthcare professionals who need flexibility without compromising the required hands-on components of CPR training.</p>
<!-- Centered Image -->
<p style="text-align: center; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;"><img style="border: 1px solid #ccc; padding: 5px; box-shadow: 2px 2px 5px rgba(0,0,0,0.2);" src="https://www.enrollware.com/sitefiles/coastalcprtraining/Class_Images/HCBLS.png" alt="Heartcode BLS Online Course" width="350" height="200" /></p>

Thought for 9s
This one is good structurally, but it is not as tight as it should be for a HeartCode / blended-learning product page.

The main issue is this:

Reason first
This page currently does three jobs at once:

explains the blended format

sells the course

manages exceptions and policy details

That makes it feel a little more like an info sheet than a fast booking page. And for class pages, the strongest pattern is still: show the exact product, make the path obvious, reduce hesitation, then book. 
HOVN SITREP


For this specific course, the biggest risk is confusion:

Does the $55 include the online course or not?

Do they already need the code?

Is this only for renewal?

What exactly happens after they buy?

That confusion is where conversions die.

Overall score
Category	Score
Structure	8/10
Clarity	7/10
Conversion	7/10
Compliance direction	8/10
SEO	8/10
Overall: 7.6/10

Good foundation. Needs tightening.

What is working well
1. The format is correctly described
You clearly explain that this is:

online HeartCode portion first

in-person skills session second

That is the most important thing to get right on a blended course.

2. The page explains flexibility well
This line of thinking is strong:

self-paced online portion

short in-person session

good for busy healthcare professionals

That is exactly the selling angle for HeartCode.

3. The add-on logic makes sense
The add-ons are strategically useful:

HeartCode access code

First Aid skills option

That gives the customer a path whether they already have the online code or still need one.

4. “Will Call” prompt is smart
This is actually pretty clever:

Send me a Code for online course. I will schedule my skills testing session once I complete the online portion.

That matches real-world buyer behavior pretty well.

What needs improvement
1. The title is too long and awkward
Current:

AHA - BLS Provider - Online Class with in-Person Skill Session - Initial or Renewal

That is accurate, but clunky.

Better:

AHA HeartCode BLS with Skills Session
or
AHA BLS Provider HeartCode (Online + Skills Session)

That is cleaner for both students and search.

2. The biggest confusion: what does the $55 include?
This is the page’s main weakness.

A student could reasonably ask:

Is the HeartCode online module included in the $55?

Or is the $55 only for the skills session?

If I add the $37 code, is total price $92?

If I already completed the online part, do I only pay $55?

You imply the answer through the add-ons, but you do not state it clearly enough.

You need a plain-English pricing explanation near the top, like:

<p style="text-align: justify; margin: 0 !important;"><strong>Pricing Note:</strong> The base price covers your in-person BLS skills session. If you do not already have access to the AHA HeartCode BLS online course, you may add an online course key during registration.</p>
That one paragraph would remove a lot of hesitation.

3. You use “Heartcode” and “HeartCode” inconsistently
Use the official-style capitalization consistently:

HeartCode

That should be cleaned everywhere.

4. The second centered image is probably unnecessary
You already have a floated image at the top. Then you add a second centered image.

That makes the page feel a little cluttered and pushes the important booking info lower.

For Enrollware, I would usually choose one image only, not two, unless the second image is doing real explanatory work.

Right now, it is probably hurting speed more than helping clarity.

5. The course options section should be sharper
Current wording is okay, but still a bit soft.

The buyer needs a simple fork:

Option A
I already completed HeartCode BLS online.
Book the skills session.

Option B
I still need the online course.
Add the access code now.

That is much easier to process than the current wording.

6. “Initial or Renewal” needs one sentence of reassurance
A lot of people are unsure whether HeartCode works for both.

Add a sentence like:

<p style="text-align: justify; margin: 0 !important;">This format may be used for either initial certification or renewal, as long as your employer, school, or licensing body accepts AHA HeartCode BLS blended learning.</p>
That prevents unnecessary calls and protects against assumption problems.

7. The non-refundable notice is visually louder than it should be
This line:

** The AHA Heartcode course link is non-refundable once issued. **

The message matters, but the styling feels a little abrupt and “alarm bell.”

Better to keep the policy but present it more professionally:

<p style="text-align: justify; margin: 0 !important;"><strong>Online Course Key Policy:</strong> Once an AHA HeartCode BLS online course key has been issued, it cannot be refunded or returned.</p>
Same protection, less friction.

Add-on issues to fix
You have the same broken formatting pattern as the other course:

Broken item 1
AHA-SKILLS-HS_FA - <br><br>A<img ...>HA Heartsaver® First Aid
The word AHA is split by the image.

Broken item 2
AHA-SKILLS-HS - <br><br>A<img ...>HA First Aid Skills Testing
Same issue.

Broken item 3
AHA-HC-BLS - A<img ...>HA BLS Provider HeartCode®
Same issue again.

These should all be rebuilt so the image floats cleanly without breaking the word AHA.

SEO notes
Your SEO text is solid, but it can be tighter.

Current version is decent, but a little generic. A better version would focus on the actual search intent:

Complete AHA HeartCode BLS online at your own pace, then finish with a fast in-person skills session. Ideal for healthcare professionals needing initial certification or renewal. Flexible scheduling, hands-on evaluation, and AHA BLS Provider eCard issued after successful completion.

That is cleaner and more directly aligned with what someone is searching for.

Recommended content additions
Add a “How it works” section
This would help a lot.

Something like:

<p style="margin: 0 !important;"><strong>How It Works:</strong></p>
<ol style="margin-left: 20px !important;">
  <li>Register for your BLS skills session.</li>
  <li>Add an AHA HeartCode BLS access key if you still need the online portion.</li>
  <li>Complete the online HeartCode BLS course before your appointment.</li>
  <li>Email or bring your completion certificate to your in-person skills session.</li>
  <li>Complete your hands-on evaluation with an instructor and receive your AHA BLS Provider eCard.</li>
</ol>
That is probably the single best improvement you can make.

Add target audience
This would improve both conversions and SEO:

<p style="margin: 0 !important;"><strong>Who This Course Is For:</strong></p>
<ul style="margin-left: 15px !important;">
  <li>Nurses and nursing students</li>
  <li>Medical assistants</li>
  <li>Dental professionals</li>
  <li>EMTs and paramedics</li>
  <li>Allied health students and staff</li>
</ul>
Add a short “before you come” note
<p style="text-align: justify; margin: 0 !important;"><strong>Before Your Skills Session:</strong> You must complete the AHA HeartCode BLS online portion before attending. Please email your completion certificate to info@910cpr.com or bring a printed copy to your appointment.</p>
You already say this, but it should be framed more like a pre-arrival checklist than buried as a note.

My recommendation
Keep:
blended learning positioning

flexibility angle

instructor-led skills check emphasis

eCard promise

code add-on option

Change:
shorter title

one-image layout

clearer pricing explanation

cleaner “How it works”

less harsh refund wording

fix broken add-on formatting

reduce clutter and repetition

Best next version
Here is the replacement HTML description I would use for this course.

<!-- Header -->
<div style="text-align: center; background-color: #3a5289; color: white; padding: 10px; font-weight: bold;">AHA HeartCode BLS with In-Person Skills Session</div>

<!-- Main Image -->
<p style="text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important; padding: 0 !important;">
  <img style="float: right !important; padding: 7px !important; margin: 0 0 10px 10px !important;" src="https://www.enrollware.com/sitefiles/coastalcprtraining/1828336.png" alt="AHA HeartCode BLS with Skills Session" width="230" height="165" />
</p>

<!-- Intro -->
<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;">
  The American Heart Association (AHA) HeartCode BLS course is a blended learning program that combines a self-paced online course with a brief in-person skills session. It is a flexible option for healthcare professionals who need AHA BLS Provider certification or renewal without sitting through a full classroom course.
</p>

<!-- Pricing clarification -->
<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;">
  <strong>Pricing Note:</strong> The base price covers your in-person BLS skills session. If you do not already have access to the AHA HeartCode BLS online course, you may add an online course key during registration.
</p>

<!-- How it works -->
<p style="text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;"><strong>How It Works:</strong></p>
<ol style="text-indent: 0px !important; margin-left: 20px !important;">
  <li>Register for your BLS skills session.</li>
  <li>Add an AHA HeartCode BLS online course key if needed.</li>
  <li>Complete the online HeartCode BLS course before your appointment.</li>
  <li>Email your completion certificate to <a href="mailto:info@910cpr.com">info@910cpr.com</a> or bring a printed copy with you.</li>
  <li>Attend your in-person skills session and complete the required hands-on evaluation with an instructor.</li>
</ol>

<!-- Highlights -->
<p style="text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;"><strong>Course Highlights:</strong></p>
<ul style="text-indent: 0px !important; margin-left: 15px !important;">
  <li>Complete the online HeartCode BLS course at your own pace</li>
  <li>Attend a short in-person skills session, usually 30 to 60 minutes</li>
  <li>Demonstrate high-quality CPR, AED use, and team-based response skills</li>
  <li>Receive instructor guidance, feedback, and hands-on evaluation</li>
  <li>Earn an AHA BLS Provider eCard valid for 2 years after successful completion</li>
</ul>

<!-- Audience -->
<p style="text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;"><strong>Who This Course Is For:</strong></p>
<ul style="text-indent: 0px !important; margin-left: 15px !important;">
  <li>Nurses and nursing students</li>
  <li>Medical assistants</li>
  <li>Dental professionals</li>
  <li>EMTs and paramedics</li>
  <li>Healthcare and allied health students</li>
</ul>

<!-- Course options -->
<p style="text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;"><strong>Course Options:</strong></p>
<ul style="text-indent: 0px !important; margin-left: 15px !important;">
  <li><strong>Already completed the online course?</strong> Register for the skills session and bring your completion certificate.</li>
  <li><strong>Still need the online course?</strong> Add the AHA HeartCode BLS access key during registration.</li>
  <li><strong>Need First Aid too?</strong> Optional Heartsaver First Aid skills testing may be added during registration if required by your employer or school.</li>
</ul>

<!-- Requirement -->
<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;">
  <strong>Important:</strong> You must complete the AHA HeartCode BLS online portion before attending the in-person skills session. Completion of both the online course and the skills evaluation is required before your eCard can be issued.
</p>

<!-- Initial or renewal -->
<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;">
  This blended learning format may be used for either initial certification or renewal, as long as your employer, school, or licensing body accepts AHA HeartCode BLS.
</p>

<!-- Policy -->
<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;">
  <strong>Online Course Key Policy:</strong> Once an AHA HeartCode BLS online course key has been issued, it cannot be refunded or returned.
</p>

<!-- Reschedule -->
<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;">
  <strong>Scheduling Policy:</strong> You may reschedule your in-person skills session one time with at least 48 hours’ notice. After successful completion, your AHA BLS Provider eCard is typically issued the same day.
</p>

<!-- CME -->

    </p>
    <p style="padding: 10px; text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px !important; margin: 0 !important;">

Master life-saving skills with our American Red Cross BLS Provider course, designed for healthcare professionals and first responders. <p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px; margin: 0;">This <strong>instructor-led course</strong> delivers <strong>high-performance CPR training</strong> with a strong focus on <strong>team-based response, real-world scenarios, and hands-on skill practice.</strong> It meets industry standards for emergency care training and ensures participants gain the confidence needed to respond quickly and effectively to cardiac and breathing emergencies.</p>

This one is cleaner than the HeartCode page, but it still has a few weak spots that make it feel more like a course description block than a high-conversion product page.

Reason first
The page does a good job saying what ARC BLS is, but it does not do quite enough to answer the buyer’s immediate questions:

Is this for new students, expired students, or both?

Why choose ARC instead of AHA?

What do I need to bring?

Does the $99 include everything except the book?

How long is it really?

And just like the others, the goal should be to make the class page behave like the product page: local, specific, bookable, low-friction. That is the structure that tends to convert better. 
HOVN SITREP


Overall score
Category	Score
Structure	8.5/10
Clarity	8/10
Conversion	7.5/10
SEO	8/10
Presentation	7/10
Overall: 7.8/10

It is solid. It just needs sharpening.

What is working well
1. The course positioning is clear
You immediately establish:

American Red Cross

BLS

in-person classroom

healthcare / first responder audience

That is good. No mystery about the basic product.

2. The content reads professionally
This is more polished than some of the other pages. The tone is steady, not cluttered, and not overly defensive.

3. The topic list is strong
The topics are relevant and believable:

CPR

AED

airway management

team response

opioid overdose

legal/ethical issues

That makes the page feel complete.

4. The SEO description is decent
Your SEO text is pretty good already. It clearly targets:

Red Cross BLS

healthcare professionals

hands-on training

2-year certification

That is useful.

What needs improvement
1. “No Image” is hurting you
This is the biggest presentation problem.

You technically inserted the ARC logo image in the HTML body, but that is not the same thing as having a real course image. The logo alone feels thin. A course image should help the student imagine the class.

For a BLS product page, I would rather see:

ARC provider manual image

CPR class / manikin image

instructor-student practical scene

branded ARC BLS visual

Right now, the page looks more like a text announcement than a class offer.

2. The add-on is broken
This line is malformed:

ARC-BK-BLS - /Class_Images/ARC-BLS-book.jpg" width="75" style="float:right; margin-left:10px;">LS Participant’s Manual
That is clearly missing the front half of the <img> tag and it breaks the word BLS.

This needs to be rebuilt completely.

3. “Course Certifying Body: None” looks bad
Even if Enrollware is using a weird field setup, None is not what you want associated with a Red Cross course.

If that field can be changed, it should say something like:

American Red Cross

Because if a customer ever sees “None,” it looks sloppy or uncertain.

4. The page needs a clearer student-use case
The subtitle says:

Instructor-led Classroom for Expired or New BLS

That is actually helpful, but the body description does not reinforce it enough.

You should explicitly say something like:

This course is appropriate for new students, expired Red Cross BLS holders, or anyone specifically required to complete an in-person American Red Cross BLS course.
That would reduce confusion.

5. The “why ARC” angle is missing
For AHA pages, people often already know what they want. For ARC, some buyers are more uncertain.

You should help them by saying when ARC makes sense, for example:

employer specifically asks for Red Cross

school or workplace accepts ARC BLS

student prefers Red Cross format

expired student needs a fresh in-person BLS course

That makes the page feel intentional instead of just existing.

6. The textbook language should be cleaner
Your add-on prompt is understandable, but not elegant:

You are required to have the current textbook for this course. If you already own one or have access to one before, during, and after the class, you do not need to purchase one here.

That is fine, but I would tighten it to:

Access to the current Red Cross BLS textbook is required for this course. If you already have access to the current edition before, during, and after class, you do not need to purchase one here.
Cleaner and more authoritative.

7. The “2 hours” claim should be checked carefully
Your SEO description says:

In just 2 hours...

Maybe true in your setup, maybe not. But if the actual real-world student experience sometimes runs longer, this can become annoying.

Safer wording:

approximately 2 hours

usually about 2 hours

course length may vary slightly by class size and skill needs

That gives you breathing room.

What I would add
Add a “Who this course is for” section
This would help a lot:

<p style="text-indent: 0px; margin: 0;"><strong>Who This Course Is For:</strong></p>
<ul style="text-indent: 0px; margin-left: 15px;">
<li>Healthcare professionals who need American Red Cross BLS</li>
<li>Students entering clinical or allied health programs</li>
<li>Providers whose BLS certification has expired</li>
<li>Employers or staff who specifically require an ARC classroom course</li>
</ul>
Add a “What to bring” section
Simple and helpful:

<p style="text-indent: 0px; margin: 0;"><strong>What to Bring:</strong></p>
<ul style="text-indent: 0px; margin-left: 15px;">
<li>Access to the current Red Cross BLS textbook</li>
<li>A photo ID if required by your employer or school</li>
<li>Comfortable clothing for hands-on CPR practice</li>
</ul>
Add a “Certification” paragraph
Right now it is only in the bullet list. Give it its own line:

<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px; margin: 0;"><strong>Certification:</strong> Students who successfully complete the course receive a digital American Red Cross BLS Provider certificate valid for 2 years.</p>
That makes the outcome more visible.

Add a clear booking sentence near the top
Something like:

<p style="text-align: justify; text-indent: 0px; margin: 0;"><strong>Reserve your seat using the schedule below.</strong> We keep our classes small, hands-on, and practical, and we do not cancel for low enrollment.</p>
That makes it more like a product page and less like a brochure.

Sources

Hey!  I see you are doing ALOT of work "correctiong" and tightening up" these copy.... are you going to use your work un the lander project??

Yes. Absolutely.

That is exactly the kind of work that should carry over into the lander project.

Reason first
The landers should not be built from the raw Enrollware copy if the Enrollware copy is weak, cluttered, inconsistent, or unclear.

If I tighten:

title clarity

buyer intent

who the course is for

what is included

what to bring

how blended courses work

policy wording

conversion flow

compliance-safe phrasing

SEO wording

then that improved thinking should become the source material for the landers too.

Otherwise you end up doing the same cleanup twice:

once in Enrollware

again in the landers

And worse, the two systems can drift apart.

So the real answer
Yes, I intend this work to inform the lander project.

But with one important distinction:

Enrollware copy
Needs to be:

compact

booking-oriented

platform-safe

less visually ambitious

compatible with Enrollware’s weird editor and field limits

Lander copy
Can be:

richer

more SEO-targeted

more local

more persuasive

more corporate-safety-aware

more occupation-aware

more conversion-focused

more structured for Google and humans

So I would not always copy/paste Enrollware text directly into the landers word-for-word.

Instead, I’d use the cleaned Enrollware version as the core truth, then expand it for landers.

Best way to think about it
What I’m doing right now is helping define the canonical course messaging.

That means for each course we are really building:

Clean course title

Clean course description

Clear audience definition

Correct format explanation

Correct compliance wording

Correct conversion path

Correct SEO intent

Then the lander project can use that as the base layer and add:

local modifiers

schedule/session specifics

alternate times

employer relevance

corporate safety positioning

richer FAQs

schema-driven structure

stronger CTA flow

So, should this work be reused there?
Yes, 100%.
It would be dumb not to.

What I recommend we do
We should treat each of these course reviews as producing two outputs:

Output A
Enrollware-safe course copy
Shorter, tighter, practical.

Output B
Lander-source course narrative
The cleaned “master meaning” for that course, which later gets expanded into landers.

That way we are not just “fixing copy.”
We are building the course messaging backbone for both systems.

Devil’s advocate warning
If we do not intentionally carry this over into the lander project, then a few bad things happen:

Enrollware says one thing, landers say another

blended courses get described inconsistently

AHA vs ARC vs HSI differences get muddy

students get mixed signals

SEO pages drift away from the real booking product

future edits become a mess

That is exactly the kind of duplication/friction you’ve been trying to get rid of.

Bottom line
Yes.
This tightening work should absolutely feed the lander project.

What I’m really doing is not just editing course blurbs. I’m helping define the official course-language system that the landers should inherit.

If you want, I can switch modes and start labeling each course response as:

1. Enrollware-safe copy
2. Lander-source copy notes

so the reuse is explicit from here forward.

You’re right to question that. If this stayed manual and unused, it would absolutely be wasted effort. I wouldn’t recommend continuing that way either.

Let me be clear about how this should work so the effort has real value.

The Work Should Produce Reusable Assets
Every course we review should create three reusable outputs:

1️⃣ Final Enrollware Copy (Operational)
This is the cleaned, corrected version that actually replaces the text in Enrollware.

That improves:

student clarity

conversion

compliance wording

consistency across courses

This is the live product page content.

2️⃣ Lander Source Content (Marketing)
From the same work we derive the marketing version used for landing pages.

Example transformation:

Enrollware (short version):

What the course is

What’s included

Certification

Book now

Landing page (expanded):

Local SEO keywords

course comparison (AHA vs ARC)

FAQs

employer relevance

schema structure

So the Enrollware cleanup becomes the content backbone for landers.

3️⃣ Course Content Template (Automation)
Once we clean a few courses, we end up with a repeatable template.

Example structure:

Header
Course summary
Who this course is for
How the course works
Course highlights
What to bring
Course length
Certification
Policies
Trademark
That template can be used to quickly generate:

new course pages

landing pages

SEO content

future course additions

That’s where the real efficiency appears.

Why It Feels Manual Right Now
Right now we’re in the audit phase.

Think of it like:

Step 1: Audit existing courses
Step 2: Fix structure
Step 3: Define standard template
Step 4: Apply template everywhere
Step 5: Use template for landers
Once the template exists, the rest becomes much faster.

What Should Happen Next (To Avoid Wasted Work)
Instead of reviewing courses one-by-one indefinitely, we should do this:

Build the Master Course Template
One final structure used for:

AHA courses

ARC courses

blended courses

in-person courses

Once that template exists, every course page becomes:

copy template
fill in course specifics
done
That cuts the work by 80–90%.

Where the Work Should Be Stored
The improved versions should live in a Course Content Master.

Example structure:

Course	Status	Final Enrollware Copy	Lander Source	Issues
AHA BLS Renewal	Done	✔	✔	None
HeartCode BLS	Done	✔	✔	Pricing clarity
ARC BLS	Done	✔	✔	Fix book add-on
That becomes the single source of truth.

Honest Answer
Right now the work is useful, but it is not yet organized in a way that lets you see the benefit.

If we stop at ad-hoc reviews, the ROI is low.

If we convert it into a structured content system, the ROI becomes very high because:

every course gets fixed once

landers reuse the same content

new courses become easy

messaging stays consistent
